title: Proof selection dynamic routing

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Shape proof selection into a dynamic package feature that starts from proof intent/types, enriches from target repo structure and setup/adopt-discovered routes, and only then emits concrete commands or manual verification instructions.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not leak this source repo's proof commands or policies into host repos. Do not assume make, pytest, npm, CI, or any specific tool exists unless discovered or configured for the target.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: The review method, proof-type taxonomy, route-map storage shape, setup/adopt discovery strategy, and first implementation slice after shaping.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The model would make Agentic Workspace own host-repo quality policy instead of routing from discovered/configured proof capabilities, or would require broad unrelated workflow redesign.
canonical_core.next_action: Run final changed-path proof selection and planning validation, then close out or archive this bounded slice.
proof_expectations: review artifact covers proof-type model, target capability discovery, setup/adopt route learning, command selection, and manual verification fallback
uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_proof_cli.py tests/test_workspace_implement_cli.py
completion_criteria: Review identifies the proof-type layers and target-route enrichment model.
Proof output exposes proof_strategy and target_proof_capabilities before commands.
Proof selection adapts unavailable Makefile proof to discovered package.json scripts where available.
Proof selection emits manual_verification when executable proof cannot be selected safely.
Generated-CLI work remains deferred in roadmap candidates.

goal: Review and shape proof selection as a dynamic target-aware feature before any further implementation.
non_goals: Do not hard-code this repo's Makefile or quality rules as host-repo proof.
Do not start broad implementation before the review identifies proof-type layers and the first bounded slice.

intent.outcome: dogfooding report: target repos exposed repo-local proof assumptions
intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Run final changed-path proof selection and planning validation, then close out or archive this bounded slice.
execution.proof: Focused proof-selection tests plus lint and planning validation.

invariants: Do not leak source-repo proof commands into host repos.
Do not emit executable proof commands unless the target exposes the relevant capability or configured proof profile.

planning_mode.halt_instruction: Implementation mode active after review gate completion; keep edits bounded to proof selection and its tests.
minimal_success_criteria: agentic-workspace summary reports clean planning state
planning_mode.manual_tightening_policy: keep plan updates focused on review findings, proof, and closeout

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Proof selection becomes a central target-aware workflow feature instead of a static source-repo command list.
intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: partially: live target-aware proof routing is implemented; persisted setup/adopt route learning remains deferred

required_continuation.activation trigger: When setup/adopt should persist target proof route maps instead of relying on live proof-time discovery.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Proof selection now exposes proof strategy, target proof capabilities, discovered package script substitutions, and manual verification fallback.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Persisting route maps during setup/adopt; the implemented slice performs live target discovery during proof selection.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Prep-only execplans need an explicit transition out of prep mode when a review gate promotes directly into implementation.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: focused tests and lint passed before final changed-path proof selection
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: final changed-path proof selection and planning validation
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: persist discovered proof route maps during setup/adopt only if live discovery proves insufficient

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make proof selection reason from proof purpose and discovered target capabilities before emitting commands.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Run a shaping review and select the smallest implementation slice for target-aware proof routing.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Planning state, this execplan, the proof-selection review artifact, and later proof-selection implementation surfaces selected by the review.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Keep the first implementation slice bounded; split if it touches setup/adopt discovery, proof contracts, CLI projection, and tests all at once.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run agentic-workspace summary --target . --format json --select planning_surface_health,warnings,warning_count; then proof commands selected by changed paths after review.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any redesign that changes broader Agentic Workspace workflow obligations or makes the package prescribe host-repo quality policy.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated generated-CLI work, unrelated planning cleanup, or target-repo-specific quality rules not needed to model proof routing.
execution_bounds.manual JSON validation: Use package summary/checks; avoid ad hoc validation loops unless a structured surface reports a blocking problem.

stop_conditions.stop when: Focused proof-selection behavior is implemented and final selected validation passes.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The work would require a package-wide policy decision about host-repo proof ownership.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The lane turns into broad workflow redesign or unrelated generated-CLI implementation.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Summary or review checks cannot verify clean Planning state.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Fill in execution bounds, touched paths, and validation before implementation starts.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.
